name: Allan Gardens Conservatory
walkingNote: Fully accessible indoor paths
permits: Free outdoors; indoor conservatory shoots require a City of Toronto commercial photography permit
parking: Metered street parking on Jarvis, paid lots within a five-minute walk
idealIf: You want tropical, Victorian-glasshouse backdrops that work rain, shine, or in deep winter.

name: Art Gallery of Hamilton
walkingNote: Sidewalk and plaza access only
permits: Contact the gallery for exterior photography; groups of ten or more are subject to a fee
parking: Street and nearby paid lots
idealIf: You want bold architectural lines and urban energy for a stylized, editorial feel.

name: Bayfront Park
vibe: Open, breezy, lakefront, relaxed, urban nature
walkingNote: Flat paved paths along the lake
permits: No permit for small shoots, check City of Hamilton for commercial bookings
parking: Free on-site lot and street parking
idealIf: You want soft lake light and city-edge greenery for a relaxed, unhurried session.

name: Casa Loma
walkingNote: Flat gardens with stairs inside the castle
permits: Paid photography permit required, $800 for a two-hour session. Book in advance with the Casa Loma events team.
parking: Limited on-site, valet and nearby public lots
idealIf: You want castle grandeur, manicured gardens, and skyline views without leaving the city.

name: Cherry Beach
walkingNote: Shoreline paths and open beach, some uneven sand
permits: City of Toronto commercial photography permit required for professional shoots
parking: Free lots near Polson Pier and Cherry Beach
idealIf: You want an unmistakably Toronto skyline across open water with a gritty, cinematic edge.

name: Distillery District
vibe: Cobblestone, industrial chic, artistic, vibrant, European
walkingNote: Flat pedestrian lanes, very walkable
permits: Private BIA property, commercial photography requires advance booking and a fee
parking: Paid on-site garages, valet options
idealIf: You want cobblestone, brick warehouses, and European old-world charm right downtown.

name: Dundas Peak
walkingNote: Reservation required, steep paths with drop-offs
permits: No photography permit, but trail reservation required via Hamilton Conservation Authority
parking: Paid lot at Spencer Gorge entry, then hike in
idealIf: You want dramatic cliff-edge views and tree-tunnel light for a destination-feel shoot without leaving Ontario.

name: Evergreen Brick Works
walkingNote: Brick paths, quarry trails, and stairs between levels
permits: Permit required for professional shoots, book through Evergreen
parking: Free on-site lot and shuttle from Broadview Station
idealIf: You want reclaimed industrial texture, brick, and quarry nature all in one frame.

name: Graffiti Alley
walkingNote: Narrow alley, five-minute stroll end to end
permits: Public laneway, small shoots usually fine. City film permit needed if blocking the lane.
parking: Metered street parking on Queen West, nearby paid lots
idealIf: You want bold, colour-saturated street art as a punchy contrast to clean styling.

name: Guild Park & Gardens
walkingNote: Paths and slopes
permits: Not required for engagement shoots or general photography
parking: Free on-site
idealIf: You want elegant, cinematic destination-feel imagery without leaving the city.

name: High Park
vibe: Seasonal, cherry blossoms, romantic, diverse backdrops
walkingNote: Expansive grounds, lake access, some hills
permits: City of Toronto commercial photography permit required for professional shoots
parking: Street parking on perimeter, small paid lots inside
idealIf: You want romantic cherry blossoms, fall colour, and lake vignettes all in one sprawling park.

name: Humber Arboretum
walkingNote: Winding trails through forest and meadow, riverside sections
permits: Free to access; contact the Arboretum for professional shoots
parking: Free parking on weekends at Humber College lots
idealIf: You want natural, romantic scenery with forest, water, and garden elements, and you don't mind a drive.

name: Humber Bay Arch Bridge
walkingNote: Flat waterfront paths, the bridge span itself is about 140 metres
permits: City of Toronto commercial photography permit required for professional shoots
parking: Free lots at Humber Bay Park East and West
idealIf: You want a sculptural, modern frame against the lake with skyline and sunset in the backdrop.

name: Kortright Centre for Conservation
walkingNote: Boardwalks and forest trails, some hills near the pond
permits: Admission paid at the gate, contact Kortright for professional bookings
parking: Free on-site parking with admission
idealIf: You want quiet forest, ponds, and boardwalks with minimal crowds for a nature-immersed session.

name: Toronto Music Garden
vibe: Whimsical, romantic, natural, garden + city mix
walkingNote: Manageable paths and hills
permits: Not needed for photos; required for civil ceremony bookings
parking: 2-minute walk away
idealIf: You prefer romantic garden aesthetics while staying downtown. Comfortable with weekend crowds.

name: Niagara Parks Botanical Gardens
walkingNote: Flat paths through 40 hectares of themed gardens
permits: No permit for small engagement shoots, weddings require a Niagara Parks booking
parking: Free on-site parking
idealIf: You want European-style garden serenity on quiet weekdays for soft, relaxed portraits.

name: Niagara-on-the-Lake
walkingNote: Easy stroll through old town streets and lakefront paths
permits: No permit for small engagement shoots, larger sessions should contact Niagara Parks
parking: Street and lot parking in town
idealIf: You want storybook old-town charm with flower boxes, colonial buildings, and lake views.

name: Osgoode Hall
vibe: Neoclassical, stately, formal, elegant, green lawns
walkingNote: Flat lawns and paths, courtyard access
permits: Law Society property, contact in advance for permission before professional shoots
parking: Paid lots nearby on Queen Street, limited street parking
idealIf: You want formal, symmetrical architecture and manicured lawns for timeless portraits.

name: Polson Pier
vibe: Panoramic skyline, waterfront, iconic, cinematic, urban
walkingNote: Flat paved pier along the harbour
permits: Private property used for events, small shoots typically fine when no event is active
parking: Free lot on-site with 100+ spaces
idealIf: You want the most iconic Toronto skyline shot from across the inner harbour.

name: Queen Victoria Park
walkingNote: Paved paths with falls vantage points, some inclines
permits: First-come first-served for outdoor photography, restricted when Niagara Parks ceremonies are running
parking: Multiple paid lots along the parkway
idealIf: You want the iconic mist, rainbows, and Horseshoe Falls as your backdrop.

name: RC Harris Water Treatment Center
walkingNote: Includes stairs and multiple spots
permits: Not needed for photography; required for vehicle access to curved road
parking: Free street parking until 4pm
idealIf: You want cinematic, romantic imagery blending architecture with waterfront scenery and don't mind walking and wind.

name: Royal Botanical Gardens
vibe: Lush, floral, serene, woodland, ever-changing blooms
walkingNote: Winding trails and woodland paths, multiple garden zones
permits: Paid photography permit required, submit request via the RBG website
parking: On-site paid lots at each garden entrance
idealIf: You want a peaceful oasis of blooms and trails that looks different every visit.

name: Scarborough Bluffs
vibe: Dramatic, coastal, wide-open, 'out of the city' feel
walkingNote: Beach access involves steep inclines
permits: None required
parking: Paid parking
idealIf: You want destination beach and cliff aesthetics without leaving Toronto. Comfortable with crowds and hiking.

name: TD Dominion Centre / King St
walkingNote: Stairs and movement between areas
permits: Not required
parking: Paid parking
idealIf: You prefer sleek, urban, downtown modern aesthetics over nature-focused styles.

name: Toronto Islands
vibe: Beachy, nostalgic, skyline views, ferry-access, relaxed
walkingNote: Ferry required, then 20-30 minutes walking between island sections
permits: City of Toronto commercial photography permit required for professional shoots
parking: Paid lots at Jack Layton Ferry Terminal, TTC recommended
idealIf: You want beaches, gardens, and the full Toronto skyline across the harbour in one session.

name: University of Toronto
walkingNote: Multiple courtyards and buildings to explore
permits: Some indoor and restricted areas require permission
parking: $10 on weekends
idealIf: You want classic, elegant European-influenced photos right in downtown Toronto with diverse backdrops.

name: Webster's Falls
walkingNote: Short trail to the viewpoint, steep sections near the base
permits: Reservation required via Hamilton Conservation Authority, no separate photography permit for small shoots
parking: Designated paid lot at the falls conservation area
idealIf: You want an intimate waterfall moment with forest trails and mist for an adventurous session.
